title: Writing: Tracing Vowels & Basic Greetings
learningGoal: Practice writing the first few Hindi vowels and constructing a polite greeting.
instructions: In this writing activity, use the provided transliteration to type out the Hindi greetings you learned. Don't worry if you don't have a Hindi keyboard; just type the romanized version (e.g., 'Namaste') or select from the word bank to build the Hindi sentence.
writingPrompt:
Translate the following into Hindi using the word bank:
1. Hello.
2. How are you?
3. I am fine.
referenceHints: In Hindi, the verb usually goes at the end of the sentence. 'You how are' -> 'Aap kaise hain'.
Don't forget the question mark for 'Aap kaise hain?'.

modelExampleOutputs.targetText: नमस्ते। आप कैसे हैं? मैं ठीक हूँ।
modelExampleOutputs.transliteration: Namaste. Aap kaise hain? Main theek hoon.
modelExampleOutputs.baseTranslation: Hello. How are you? I am fine.

evaluationCriteria.description: Forms 'Aap kaise hain' in the correct order.

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: नमस्ते। आप कैसे हैं? मैं ठीक हूँ।
acceptableAlternates: Namaste. Aap kaise hain? Main theek hoon.
namaste. aap kaise hain? main theek hoon.
adminCorrectAnswerSet.grammarBaseline: Accepts transliteration for absolute beginners. Correct word order is mandatory.

criteria.name: Vocabulary Selection
criteria.description: Selected the right words for Hello, You, How, Are.

feedbackRules.onPass: Excellent! You can now write basic greetings in Hindi.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review the word order. In Hindi, it's 'You how are' (Aap kaise hain).
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good try! Make sure you matched 'Main' (I) with 'hoon' (am) and 'Aap' (You) with 'hain' (are).

hintText: Match the English words to the transliteration in the word bank to build the sentence.
commonMistakes: Writing 'Aap hain kaise' instead of 'Aap kaise hain'.
